title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence · Proof Workshop
summary: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
